title: erigeron ramosus subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Erigeron ramosus subsp. septentrionalis, also known as beaked daisy or beaked fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Erigeron ramosus subsp. septentrionalis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
